---
title: "Introduction"
subtitle: "Introduction to Big Data in the Social Sciences"
author: Laurence-Olivier M. Foisy
institute: Université de Montréal
lang: en
from: markdown+emoji
format:
  revealjs:
    mermaid: 
      theme: dark
    theme: simple
    logo: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/4/4b/Universite_de_Montreal_logo.svg/1920px-Universite_de_Montreal_logo.svg.png
    footer: "[FAS1001](https://fas1001.com)"
    transition: slide
    transition-speed: fast
    code-fold: false
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## Your Instructor {.biggest}

- Laurence-Olivier M. Foisy
- Email: [mail@mfoisy.com](mailto:mail@mfoisy.com)
- Best way to reach me: Slack
- Office: None
- University: Laval 

## My Journey in Data Science {.smaller}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

![](https://www.clessn.com/assets/img/logo-black.svg)

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

- Master's in Peace Studies in Japan, 100% qualitative
- Started PhD in 2023 
- Meeting with the director of CLESSN
- Exposure to the world of data science
- Participation in several research projects

:::

::::

## Questions for You 

- Your study programs?
- Your knowledge of R?
- Do you use generative AI?
- Your knowledge of databases?
- Have you ever scraped data from the web?
- Why did you take this course?

# Course Presentation

## What is Big Data? {auto-animate=true}

### The Three Vs? 

- Volume
- Velocity
- Variety

## The Three Vs?

![](https://www.coforge.com/hubfs/Imported_Blog_Media/The-3Vs-of-big-data-1-1.png){.absolute width="50%" .fragment .fade-in}

## The Four Vs?

![](https://opensistemas.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/4-Vs-of-big-data-1.jpg){.absolute width="80%" .fragment .fade-in}

## The Five Vs?

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:720/format:webp/1*YejjU_69ffDyrC0z-X9jYQ.jpeg){.absolute width="100%" .fragment .fade-in}

## The Six Vs?

![](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Javier-Andreu-Perez/publication/280124446/figure/fig2/AS:284445564260357@1444828515809/Six-Vs-of-big-data-value-volume-velocity-variety-veracity-and-variability-which.png){.absolute width="60%" .fragment .fade-in}

## The Seven Vs?

![](https://impact.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/The-7vs-of-big-data.png){.absolute width="70%" .fragment .fade-in}

## The Eight Vs?

![](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Ijaz-Ahmad-27/publication/368766798/figure/fig2/AS:11431281122255075@1677251046475/Characteristics-of-Big-Data-with-8Vs-that-support-InX-Big-Data-management.ppm){.absolute width="50%" .fragment .fade-in}

## The Nine Vs?

![](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Bihter-Das-2/publication/350075342/figure/fig1/AS:1001705609109517@1615836636962/characteristics-of-big-data.png){.absolute width="60%" .fragment .fade-in}

## The Ten Vs?

![](https://datasciencedojo.com/wp-content/uploads/10-vs-of-big-data-1030x1030.png.webp){.absolute width="60%" .fragment .fade-in}

## The Eleven Vs?

![](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/356019405/figure/fig1/AS:1088172826066952@1636452027036/The-main-characteristics-of-big-data-the-11-Vs.jpg){.absolute width="40%" .fragment .fade-in}

## The Twelve Vs?

![](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Ernane-Martins-2/publication/335105621/figure/fig2/AS:790671002726400@1565522065239/The-12-Vs-in-Big-Data-Font-Adapted-by-author-2018.png){.absolute width="60%" .fragment .fade-in}

## The Thirteen Vs?

![](https://clipart-library.com/images/gie5B478T.png){.absolute width="60%" .fragment .fade-in}


## The Fourteen Vs?

![](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Bazlur-Rashid/publication/322247301/figure/fig1/AS:579058937614339@1515069817678/Big-Data-technology-with-14-Vs.png){.absolute width="65%" .fragment .fade-in}

## The Fifteen Vs?

![](https://clipart-library.com/images/gie5B478T.png){.absolute width="60%" .fragment .fade-in}


## How Many "Vs" Can Be Integrated into a Concept? {.biggest}

::: {.r-fit-text}
Answer: 14!
:::

::: {.callout-important}
### The Real Question
It's not "How many Vs?", but "How to use this data in a responsible and relevant way?"
:::

## Another Definition: The 5Ws? {auto-animate=true}

- Who?
- What?
- When?
- Where?
- Why?

## Another Definition: The 5Ws? {auto-animate=true}

- Who?
- What?
- When?
- Where?

## Another Definition: The 5Ws? {auto-animate=true}

- Why?

## Another Definition: The 5Ws? {auto-animate=true}

- Why?
  - Doing science?
  - Understanding the world around us?
  - Making informed decisions?
  - Because it's fun?

## Which Dataset Yields the Best Results? {auto-animate=true}

- A representative survey of 1,000 participants?
- 2M Facebook comments?

## Which Dataset Yields the Best Results? {auto-animate=true}

It depends on the research question!

- What is the social acceptability of the US kidnapping Maduro?

- What is the effect of driving a truck or an electric car on voting intentions? 

## Before and Now {.smaller}

**Before**: Data was created specifically to answer a research question.

**Now**: Existing data is used to answer a research question.

One is not better than the other. They meet different needs.

## Everyday Data Becomes Research Data {.smaller}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Original Data**

- Reddit discussions
- Strava heatmaps
- Spotify playlists
- LinkedIn profiles
- TikTok trends
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Research Questions**

- Public opinion on AI?
- Post-pandemic urban mobility?
- Generational cultural identities?
- Gendered professional trajectories?
- Diffusion of social movements?
:::

::::


:::{.callout-important}
The goal of this course is to teach you to see everyday data as potential research data.
:::

## The Fundamental Principle of Big Data

### Repurposing

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

![](img/abstraction.png)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
Our work

![](https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/3808/3808508.png){.absolute top=200 right=0 width="40%"}

:::
::::


## Course Structure

![](img/structure_cours_en.png)

## Objectives

- Develop a global understanding of the various data sources available for studying social phenomena.

- Develop the autonomy required to collect, manage, and quantitatively analyze databases, and integrate them into a research project.

- Demonstrate an understanding of the issues related to mobilizing and using large databases in the social sciences.

## Tools to Get There {.smaller}

![](img/outils_numeriques_2025.jpg)

## Evaluations {.smaller}

| Evaluations                     | Points  | Dates                        |
|---------------------------------|---------|------------------------------|
| Assignment 1 - Introduction     | 10%     | January 28, 2026 before midnight |
| Quiz 1                          | 15%     | February 5, 2026             |
| Assignment 2 - Midterm project  | 20%     | March 11, 2026 before midnight   |
| Quiz 2                          | 15%     | April 9, 2026                |
| Assignment 3 - Term project     | 30%     | April 30, 2026 before midnight   |
| Class participation             | 10%     | January 8 to April 30        |
| Total                           | 100%    | January 8 to April 30        |

## Course Website

The course website is available at [https://fas1001.com](https://fas1001.com).
All information regarding the course will be published there, including slides, assignments, quizzes, the syllabus, and important dates.

## Quick Notes: {.smaller}

- ChatGPT?
- Late submission rules: 10% per day
- Required material
- Have questions? Ask them!
- Have comments? Share them!
- Have suggestions? Propose them!

# Have a great semester!
